Chapter 17. Corn (PDF, 766 KB)

On this page

  • Corn, Canada Western Yellow, White or Mixed (CW)
  • Corn, Canada Eastern Yellow, White or Mixed (CE)

Corn, Canada Western Yellow, White or Mixed (CW)

Print format
Download a poster version of the primary and export grade determination table for Corn, Canada Western Yellow, White or Mixed (CW), 61 x 91 cm (24 x 36 in) (PDF, 173 KB)

The colour is added to the grade name.

Corn, Canada Western Yellow, White or Mixed (CW), standard of quality
Grading factor No. 1 CW No. 2 CW No. 3 CW No. 4 CW No. 5 CW Grade, if No. 5 specs not met
Degree of soundness Cool and sweet, uniform size Cool and sweet Cool and sweet Cool and sweet May have a slight odour, not sour or musty  
Minimum test weight kg/hL (g/0.5 L) 68 (343) 66 (333) 64 (322) 62 (311) 58 (291) Corn, Sample CW (class) Account Light Weight
Variety Any variety of corn Any variety of corn Any variety of corn Any variety of corn Any variety of corn  
Other classes % 5 5 5 5 5 Over 5%—Use all other grading criteria and grade as Corn (grade) Mixed
Corn, Canada Western Yellow, White or Mixed (CW), damage
Grading factor No. 1 CW No. 2 CW No. 3 CW No. 4 CW No. 5 CW Grade, if No. 5 specs not met
Heated % 0.1 0.2 0.5 1.0 3.0 Corn, Sample CW (class) Account Heated
Total %
Damage		 3 5 7 10 15 Corn, Sample CW (class) Account Damaged
Corn, Canada Western Yellow, White or Mixed (CW), foreign material
Grading factor No. 1 CW No. 2 CW No. 3 CW No. 4 CW No. 5 CW Grade, if No. 5 specs not met
Excreta % 0.02 0.02 0.02 0.02 0.02 Corn, Sample CW (class) Account Excreta
Total %
Cracked corn and foreign material		 2 3 5 7 12 50% or less – Corn Sample CW (class) Account CCFM Over 50% - sample Cracked Corn and Foreign Material
StonesFootnote 1 % 0.15 0.15 0.15 0.15 0.15 2.5% or less—Corn, Rejected (grade) (class) Account Stones
Over 2.5%—Corn, Sample Salvage
Table 1 Notes
Footnote 1

Stones are not included in Total cracked corn and foreign material

Return to footnote 1 referrer

Corn, Canada Eastern Yellow, White or Mixed (CE)

Print format
Download a poster version of the primary and export grade determination table for Corn, Canada Eastern Yellow, White or Mixed (CE), 61 x 91 cm (24 x 36 in) (PDF, 173 KB)

The colour is added to the grade name.

Corn, Canada Eastern Yellow, White or Mixed (CE), standard of quality
Grading factor No. 1 CE No. 2 CE No. 3 CE No. 4 CE No. 5 CE Grade, if No. 5 specs not met
Degree of soundness Cool and sweet, uniform size Cool and sweet Cool and sweet Cool and sweet May have a slight odour, not sour or musty  
Minimum test weight kg/hL (g/0.5 L) 68 (343) 66 (333) 64 (322) 62 (311) 58 (291) Corn, Sample CE (class) Account Light Weight
Variety Any variety of corn Any variety of corn Any variety of corn Any variety of corn Any variety of corn  
Other classes % 5 5 5 5 5 Over 5%—Use all other grading criteria and grade as Corn (grade) Mixed
Corn, Canada Eastern Yellow, White or Mixed (CE), damage
Grading factor No. 1 CE No. 2 CE No. 3 CE No. 4 CE No. 5 CE Grade, if No. 5 specs not met
Heated % 0.1 0.2 0.5 1.0 3.0 Corn, Sample CE (class) Account Heated
Total %
Damage		 3 5 7 10 15 Corn, Sample CE (class) Account Damaged
Corn, Canada Eastern Yellow, White or Mixed (CE), foreign material
Grading factor No. 1 CE No. 2 CE No. 3 CE No. 4 CE No. 5 CE Grade, if No. 5 specs not met
Excreta % 0.02 0.02 0.02 0.02 0.02 Corn, Sample CE (class) Account Excreta
Total %
Cracked corn and foreign material		 2 3 5 7 12 50% or less – Corn Sample CE (class) Account CCFM Over 50% - sample Cracked Corn and Foreign Material
StonesFootnote 1 % 0.15 0.15 0.15 0.15 0.15 2.5% or less—Corn, Sample CE (class) Account Stones Over 2.5%—Corn, Sample Salvage
Table 1 Notes
Footnote 1

Stones are not included in Total cracked corn and foreign material
